note: The living-state spine — every roadmap item + the PR/status that shipped it (see livingState below for the full sync rule). Read by lib/hermes/statusBoard.ts to generate STATUS.md + the STATUS blocks in CLAUDE.md/README.md/BUILD_LOG.md. See brain/README.md for the full brain/ folder index.
northStar: The AI-engine pillar of WIFI DJ (wifidj.xyz). The lyrical process + the brain are the moat — not the video studio.
principles: no living-artist mimicry — influences are felt, never copied
assistant, not autopilot — the artist decides
real-AI and Web3 are opt-in, behind adapters/APIs, never bloating the free local core

livingState.note: One source of truth so nothing goes stale or gets left behind.
livingState.capture: IDEAS.md — the capture net; nothing the artist says is lost
livingState.spine: brain/roadmap.json — this file; indexes every item + the PR that shipped it
livingState.rule: Every PR updates the spine + TODO + IDEAS + README together. Shipping an item flips its status here and moves it to Shipped in TODO. A PR that skips this is not done.

tractionSprint.note: Ranked $0 items from the 3-agent GitHub-traction audit. Theme: make display-only brain systems load-bearing, wire in vector memory, document the architecture.

items.title: Vector-memory determinism hardening
Semantic originality — meaning-level novelty (optional, server-side)
Close the cognition loop — cognition picks the hook + regenerate-from-critiques
Interactive trace explorer + demo gallery
Council scoring → hook ranking + learn→vector recall
ARCHITECTURE.md + brain-wiring diagram (generated from brainMap)

phases.name: Living-state spine
phases.goal: A single machine-readable roadmap the /loop reads each iteration; keep all four tracking surfaces in lockstep.

items.title: brain/roadmap.json spine + sync discipline
items.note: the spine is live and every PR syncs it (living-state rule); drift now CI-enforced by 0.4

items.title: Refresh TODO.md to reality (mark #30–#34 shipped)
items.note: TODO refreshed and kept newest-first since; staleness audit 2026-07-02 re-verified

items.title: CLAUDE.md memory spine — saved conventions + routing table, guard-tested
items.note: lib/hermes/__tests__/claudeMd.test.ts keeps the routes honest; maintenance rule: repeat an instruction twice → save it

items.title: Status Board — spine-generated status tables + drift tests (never-stale checklists)
items.note: STATUS.md + STATUS marker blocks in CLAUDE.md/README/BUILD_LOG are GENERATED from this file; statusBoard.test.ts fails CI on drift, bans hand checkboxes outside the allowlist, and closes the wiring/personas generated-doc drift hole. Regenerate: GEN_DOCS=1 npx vitest run status

items.title: Branch ledger — every branch cross-referenced against its PR + merge status
items.note: Founder ask: audit every branch, confirm nothing got missed or left unmerged, and give it a memory layer. Full audit (unshallowed the repo first — the sandbox clone was shallow at exactly 50 commits, which silently faked every ahead/behind count) found 111 remote branches and 169 PRs, cross-referenced via the GitHub API: 100% merged, zero open PRs, zero orphan branches with no PR, zero closed-unmerged PRs. brain/branches.json is the ledger (per-branch lastCommit/aheadOfMain + its PR list + status); scripts/branch-ledger.mjs refreshes the git-derived fields on demand ($0/local, no GitHub token) — PR/status fields are filled in by hand during the next periodic audit, the same findings-only-tooling boundary the watchdog already draws.

phases.name: Measure & make it safe
phases.goal: Cheap, high-credibility foundation that makes everything after it measurable and responsible.

items.title: Eval harness + golden songs (npm run eval)
items.note: objective metrics (rhyme density, line diversity, thematic coherence, hook strength) over the demos+flagship golden set; regression guard in CI

items.title: Output-safety filter + short disclaimer/ToS
items.note: screens hooks/lyrics against a famous-phrase list → a 'famous-phrase' uniqueness flag; disclaimer in README + Uniqueness panel

items.title: One-command demo (npm run demo) — full song end-to-end
items.note: generates a full original song + prints the 11-region generation trace; lyrics-focused, no video; deterministic

items.title: LAUNCH.md kit (star checklist + Twitter thread + demo-recording script)
items.note: rewrote the stale video-first kit into a brain-first launch playbook: pre-flight checklist, demo-recording script, draft X thread, channel sequence (X → Show HN → Reddit → PH → YouTube), guardrails

phases.name: Deepen the visible brain
phases.goal: The lyrics/brain focus, made visible — the star magnet.

items.title: Cognitive model: first thought → second thought → decision
items.note: deliberate() runs 3 real critiques (true-to-brief / original / earns-it) → keep|revise; a '🧭 How the brain decided' readout on the lead hook

items.title: The Council — agents as a deliberating board
items.note: right hemisphere proposes / left challenges / artist decides; reuses agent findings + cognition deliberate(). The Crossroads Board made literal. Also added a .env* gitignore guard + .env.example (RUNWAY/ANTHROPIC placeholders) so a founder key can never be committed.

items.title: Brain-scan boot sequence + live per-agent drive
items.note: a scan-line boot sweep while running (reduced-motion aware) + regions/agents ignite in sequence live off the per-agent onProgress stream. COMPLETES PHASE 2.

items.title: Particle Brain + artist heat-map (living Brain Scan)
items.note: SHIPPED (founder idea): canvas particle layer over the SVG brain + a thermal heat-map; brainHeat() (tested) colors regions by artist type (right/generative=hot magenta, analytical=cyan, emotion raises temp, more 'becoming-you'=hotter). Reduced-motion aware. Header shows the artist type + brain temp. Feeds the Living-Brain dNFT.

items.title: Deep Brain Atlas — 37 anatomy-named subregions, each mapped to a real module
items.note: constellation expansion in BrainScan + per-subsection trace notes + atlas tables in docs/brain-wiring.md; honesty rule enforced by brainAtlas.test.ts

items.title: Council build plan, PR1 — your learned taste as a 4th voice
items.note: A research agent audited council.ts/Council.tsx and found the board's ranking was display-only, disconnected from the artist's own learned Taste vault even though becomingYou.ts's voiceMirror already scores whole songs against it. voiceFit(hookText, taste) scores a single hook the same way; rankHooksByCouncil() takes taste as an optional 4th voice, active only once taste.edits > 0 (byte-identical ranking otherwise). 4 more PRs queued (see IDEAS.md): Council verdicts as real Crossroads crossings, Council judging across vault takes, a hookFinale field the Council's vote actually steers, and live deliberation in the Lyric Lab.

items.title: Agent Network codenames — Council companion identities
items.note: Founder-supplied sneak-peek box-art (assets/concept-art/wifi-dj-agent-network-sneak-peek.png) introduced 7 named Agent Network companions (Synapse, Vylo, Rhythmix, Lumi, Echo, Harmony, Drifter). Confirmed via AskUserQuestion this should reskin the real 10-agent pipeline, not the 6 lyric personas or Council.tsx's guest judges/agent packs. Shipped as a display-only codename field on AgentDefinition (zero determinism-contract risk — name stays the string baked into committed example fixtures and generated suggestedNextAction text): Nexus (Conductor), Synapse (Hooksmith), Vylo (Lyric Chemist), Rhythmix (Beat Oracle), Echo (Emotion Scanner), Sentinel (Originality Auditor), Harmony (A&R Judge), Lumi (Visual Director), Drifter (Viral Clip Scout), Beacon (Rights & Release Guard). Council.tsx shows the codename with the original functional name alongside it. Unblocks docs/runway-plan.md Phase 3 (agent avatars), which was waiting on exactly this naming decision. The art itself is concept/pitch-only per founder call — saved as reference, not wired into the live hero/OG card.

items.title: AgentAvatar — $0 SVG glyphs for the Agent Network codenames
items.note: Follow-on to 2.7: the real avatar-images TODO item is founder-gated (Grok/Gen-4-image stills, plus RUNWAY_API_KEY for animation), so shipped the buildable-now $0 slice instead — a small deterministic SVG glyph per codename (line-art in BrainScan.tsx's existing stroke style, tinted by hemisphere via currentColor, no image asset/API key/network call). AgentCodename moved to types.ts (was inline in agents.ts) so AgentDefinition.codename and AgentAvatar's glyph map share one type — tsc itself now guarantees every codename has a glyph (Record<AgentCodename, JSX.Element> fails to compile if one is missing). Wired into Council.tsx next to each codename label. Screenshot-verified live: all 10 glyphs render distinctly, correctly tinted per hemisphere, no console errors.

phases.name: Make it yours (the living world)
phases.goal: The brain becomes the individual artist over time.

items.title: Create-your-own-artist v1 + Story Mode
items.note: deriveArtist() grows an identity (alias, signature words, hemisphere, chapter, bio) from vault+taste; Story Mode chapters (First Spark → Finding Your Voice → First Banger → The Album) unlock on songCount/becomingYou/bestScore. A '🎭 Your Artist' card (editable, persisted alias) shows the bio + chapter + next unlock.

items.title: Community-authored personas (craft-DNA, like scene packs)
Pro Studio Rack — modular upgradeable engine 'boxes' (Pro Tools aesthetic)
items.note: SHIPPED (founder idea): a DAW-style '🎛️ Engine Rack' — the free Local Combinator ACTIVE + locked Claude/Lightning upgrade slots ('connect a key/server to unlock') on the LyricsProvider seam. Premium units light up when the founder connects keys/servers.

items.title: HERMES Studio workspace (Suno-Studio-style: section timeline + rack + meter bridge)
items.note: SHIPPED (this PR, founder-supplied Suno Studio screenshots as reference): a read-only 🎚️ HERMES Studio panel — the song's real sections as a clip timeline (clip width a rough length estimate, the same 2-bars/line-at-tempo rule of thumb SongPackageView already uses; not a measurement), a clickable per-clip lyric preview, and a 'meter bridge' reading the real 11-region brain state (REGIONS + regionState from brainMap.ts) as a mixing-desk-style row. Wired as a 5th Studio Flow rail tab in HermesHitFactory.tsx; activating it ring-highlights this panel alongside the already-existing Brain Scan and Engine Rack panels rather than re-mounting duplicate instances of either (BrainScan runs a live particle canvas — a second copy would double that animation everywhere the page renders). Clip editing stays a later step.

items.title: Bring Your Own Sound, PR1 — Voice Notes: record & attach a take
items.note: FOUNDER: record your voice/an instrument, hum a melody to shape the flow. PR1 ships the recording plumbing: MediaRecorder capture, IndexedDB storage (audioVault.ts, in-memory fallback mirroring storage.ts's kv()), play/delete, wired into SongPackageView. Zero new deps, zero effect on generation — pure attachment. PR2 (instrument riff clips) and PR3 (hum-to-flow: a deterministic flowContour syllable/stress target feeding mockLyricsProvider's line-filler) queued — see IDEAS.md for the full phased plan and the scope call (rhythm-matching, not full melody transcription).

items.title: Bring Your Own Sound — upload an existing audio file (not just live mic recording)
items.note: SHIPPED (this PR, exactly the predicted shape): an <input type="file" accept="audio/*"> "⬆️ Upload a sound" button beside "🎙️ Record a take" in VoiceNotes.tsx, feeding the same audioVault.ts saveClip() the mic path proved out — saved as a 'riff' clip (already a supported kind, already unit-tested), sharing the 6-clip cap, size cap, IndexedDB store, and player. Duration read off a throwaway <audio> element's loadedmetadata (falls back to 0 for formats that report Infinity/NaN); label from the filename; clip rows show 🎵 (uploaded) vs 🎙️ (recorded). $0/no-new-deps (File API is a browser built-in), zero effect on generation — pure attachment. Playwright-verified end to end with a real WAV upload → playable 🎵 clip. PR3 (hum-to-flow) still queued.

phases.name: Durability
phases.goal: Stop data loss; a cleared browser must never wipe a catalog.

items.title: Vault durability — export-on-change + file/cloud backup
items.note: every write mirrors to a .bak key; Vault drawer shows backup status + one-tap restore (#298 surfaced it). The optional file/cloud lane stays 4.2.

items.title: Optional durable cloud brain (Notion/Drive backing)

phases.name: Real intelligence (opt-in)
phases.goal: The smallest real-AI increment that feels transformative; mock stays default so the core stays $0.

items.title: claudeLyricsProvider behind ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (mock default)
items.note: Provider (Messages API, structured JSON, call-time key, typed errors) + evalCompare.ts + gated eval:compare lane shipped; mock stays default, nothing client-side imports it, zero live calls made. Live comparison run = founder-triggered: locally via RUN_LIVE_EVAL=1 ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=... npm run eval:compare, or from a phone via the manual claude-compare GitHub Action (key in Actions secrets). See docs/claude-engine.md.

items.title: Rhyme/BPM validation loop on generated output
Vector memory + semantic search (local embeddings, opt-in)
items.note: FOUNDER prompt. A local semantic-search layer on top of the rule/lexicon systems (never replaces them). @xenova/transformers is an OPTIONAL lazy dep. Pure cosine-search core unit-tested WITHOUT the model. #49 DETERMINISM HARDENING: sort by a quantized rank (similarity×1e8) + deterministic tie-break (id, then text) so search is reproducible across Intel/Apple-Silicon/AMD despite FP/BLAS variance; stored embeddings are the reproducible source of truth. Next: wire into originality (traction #1).

items.title: Claude Engine BYOK panel — visitor's own key, browser-only
items.note: FOUNDER prompt: wire Claude into the live panel now that a key exists. The only design consistent with the $0/no-server laws: the visitor pastes THEIR OWN Anthropic key into the Engine Rack; it lives only in their browser's localStorage (lib/hermes/claudeKey.ts) and their browser calls api.anthropic.com directly (anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access header, Anthropic's sanctioned BYOK escape hatch). No founder key, no proxy, no server ever involved — satisfies SECURITY.md's proxy/rate-limit/spend-cap rule by having no proxy at all. Reproduced/shared songs always use the mock (byte-identical for every viewer). On any Claude failure, the app falls back to the free engine for that take with an honest banner. See docs/claude-engine.md → 'Run it from the panel'.

items.title: Scribe line editor + Rack 'Test key' verification
items.note: FOUNDER prompt: 'make sure Claude API is working, people should be able to edit their lyrics almost like the application SCRIBE.' (1) ScribeEditor.tsx replaces the single-textarea lyric edit with per-line fields (add/delete/reorder-by-position), each with a ✨ AI-rewrite control that calls the new suggestLineRewrites() (Claude Engine, 3 in-context alternate phrasings) when the visitor's own BYOK key is unlocked+active, else shows an honest unlock hint instead of hiding the button. The old raw-text textarea stays one tap away ('edit as raw text') for power-user paste workflows — no regression. Both funnel through the same renderSections()/saveLyricEdit()/learn-from-edits path. (2) The Rack gained a 'Test key' button — an explicit, opt-in, real minimal round-trip (testClaudeKey(), 16 max_tokens) against api.anthropic.com so a visitor can confirm their pasted key actually works before generating with it; never called automatically. claudeLyricsProvider.ts refactored to a shared callClaudeMessages() so generation, line rewrites, and the key test share one code path for key resolution/CORS header/error typing. Verified live with Playwright: locked-hint popover, line edit/add, raw-text round trip, save-teaches-brain banner, and the Test-key button failing gracefully (invalid test key) without crashing.

items.title: Pattern packs — rhyme-scheme + form variety ($0/local)
items.note: FOUNDER prompt: 'lyrics are all coming out very similar in regards to pattern... people should be able to choose more.' Grounded in a /deep-research pass (104 agents, 22 sources, 9 verified findings). Two real gaps fixed: (1) rhyme generation was hard-coded to sequential AABB couplets — buildRhymedVerse() in mockLyricsProvider.ts now takes a rhymeScheme dial (AABB/ABAB/ABBA/AAAA/XAXA) via a family-per-line layout, reusing rhyme.ts's already-generic rhymeFamily(n) hook; verified two ways in patternPacks.test.ts (byte-identical reproduction per seed, and the EXISTING scoring-side rhymeScheme() detector independently confirms each generated verse reads back as the scheme requested). (2) the 'full-song' structure option was a silent duplicate of 'hook-first' (same default case) — now rides out on a repeated final Hook, in the spirit of the verified AABA convention ('no new lyrics after the first cycle', OMT/Summach MTO 17.3). brain/patternPacks.json bundles structure+rhymeScheme into named presets (AABA Classic, Ballad/XAXA, Verse-Chorus Alternating/ABAB, Monorhyme Chant, Enclosed/ABBA, Strophic-lean) with honest sourceNotes distinguishing verified findings (AABA rule, section-function semantics, genre priors) from the rhyme-scheme dial itself (a general, well-established poetic device offered without an unverified genre-mapping claim — see docs/pattern-packs.md's 'what's research-backed' section). New SongLabForm 'Pattern pack' quick-apply dropdown + a standalone 'Rhyme scheme' dial. Deferred: meter/stress and rap-flow parameters (no syllable-aware generation infra yet) — captured in TODO.md/IDEAS.md backlog. Demos regenerated (GEN_DEMOS=1); default (rhymeScheme unset) still AABB, byte-identical scheme detection to before.

items.title: Watchdog — Claude-powered security/quality review, scheduled + findings-only
items.note: FOUNDER prompt (via /goal): 'an agent... consistently monitoring the system, finding weaknesses, also finding ways to improve the system through research... deploy its own developer agent, security code review... run on a dynamic type loop through the Claude API.' Shipped in two passes. Pass 1: a manual (workflow_dispatch) Claude review over a bounded, curated context (last 20 commits, npm audit, CLAUDE.md/SECURITY.md in full, every security-sensitive file + .github/workflows/*.yml) -> structured findings + research ideas filed as a GitHub issue (watchdog-report). Pass 2 (this /goal follow-up): built an auto-fix-PR follow-on (scripts/watchdog-fix.mjs — pick a low-severity/high-confidence finding, draft a one-file Claude patch, validate against the full gate suite, auto-commit+push+open a draft PR) — the platform's own auto-mode safety classifier BLOCKED wiring this live, flagging unattended code-write-and-push with no human-approval step as a real risk boundary. Given the choice (AskUserQuestion), the founder chose: drop the auto-fix-PR piece entirely, keep it findings-only PERMANENTLY (not a v1 gap), and add the `schedule:` trigger (weekly, Mondays) for genuine unattended monitoring — safe specifically because the workflow's ceiling stays issues:write, never contents:write, so an unattended run can spend money but cannot alter the repo. SECURITY.md/CLAUDE.md's Actions-secret rules rewritten: workflow_dispatch-only is the default; schedule: is a named, one-at-a-time exception, gated on the workflow being structurally incapable of writing code. See docs/watchdog.md for the full before/after reasoning, including why the reverted auto-fix design isn't 'deferred' but genuinely scrapped.

items.title: Occasion Packs — holiday/life-moment lexicon + dedication (Song Gifts, phase 1)
items.note: FOUNDER idea (holiday song packs) + Fable 5's paired pitch for the sell around it (Song Gifts — a song written FOR someone, delivered as the existing deterministic share link). Phase 1 ships the engine: brain/occasionPacks.json (Christmas, Valentine's, Mother's/Father's Day, Birthday, Anniversary, Graduation, New Year) — each a mood/genre/structure/rhymeScheme preset PLUS a genuinely new imagery-cluster vocabulary (stocking, mistletoe, diploma, tassel...) that mood/genre/references text alone can't express, so `occasion` is its own SongInputs field (unlike pattern packs) validated at all three untrusted-input boundaries — pipeline normalize, share decode, vault import — same discipline as rhymeScheme. The mock provider biases noun slots toward the pack's vocabulary (nounPool/imageryNouns) and swaps the Intro line for the pack's dedication frame ('Merry Christmas, {who}') when set. Surfaced and fixed a real pre-existing bug along the way: third-person pronouns (she/he/they/our...) were absent from BOTH the keyword-extraction stopword list and the noun-slot exclusion list, so a natural dedication theme ('everything she gave') leaked 'she'/'our' into noun slots ('through the she') — fixed at both layers, caught by testing the feature's own flagship use case (a Mother's Day dedication) before shipping. SongLabForm gets an Occasion picker that also relabels Audience to 'Dedicated to (name)'. Demos regenerated (hometown-ghosts shifted — a legitimate noun-pool dedup fix, not a regression; golden eval stays 6/6 PASS). Song Gifts phase 2 (a dedicated gifting flow + gift-framed share card) captured in IDEAS.md, queued next.

items.title: Song Gifts — gift-framed share link, PNG card, and OG unfurl (phase 2)
items.note: Completes the Song Gifts pitch from 5.8: whenever a package carries an Occasion Pack + a dedicated audience name, every existing share surface becomes gift-aware instead of gaining new ones. shareLink.ts's new giftMessage() turns the copied clipboard text into a one-line gift message ('🎄 A Christmas song for Mom — open it to watch the brain write it: <url>') instead of a bare URL; SongPackageView's Share button becomes '🎄 Share the gift' and copies that message. Opening a gift link shows a themed 'gift reveal' banner (HermesHitFactory) before the brain scan — '🎄 A Christmas song, written for Mom'. The downloadable PNG card (shareCard.ts's new giftEyebrow()) and the env-gated OG unfurl (functions/_lib/ogCard.ts, still OG_UNFURL=1-gated + inert by default) both swap their eyebrow/title/description to the gift framing too, so a gift link previews correctly in iMessage/Slack/Discord once the founder activates unfurl. +~30 tests across shareCard/shareLink/ogFunction. Playwright-verified live end to end: generated a Christmas gift for 'Mom', copied the gift message, opened it in a fresh page, watched the reveal banner render — zero console errors either side. FOLLOW-UP (same session): the PNG card's giftEyebrow() work surfaced that renderShareCard/downloadShareCard had no UI trigger at all — fixed with a '🖼 Download card' button in SongPackageView, verified to produce a real ~700KB PNG.

phases.name: Influence Studio (WIFI DJ framework Part 1)
phases.goal: Describe an influence (felt, never copied) → craft parameters.

items.title: Influence Studio — thematic cartography + lexical fingerprinting

phases.name: Tiny features — the standing cadence
phases.goal: Founder directive, 2026-07-03: keep shipping small, self-contained features that each amplify one little area — one green-loop PR at a time, no fixed end. Paused by the founder after 7.25 (25 shipped) in favor of planned medium-sized features — see phase 8.

items.title: 🎲 Surprise me — a varied starter-brief pool for Song Lab
items.note: loadExample() only ever loaded one fixed EXAMPLE_BRIEF; a new STARTER_BRIEFS pool (6 varied genre/mood/structure/rhyme-scheme combos) plus a 'Surprise me' button gives a blank-page visitor real variety, never a same-brief repeat click. Pure UI convenience, zero pipeline/determinism impact.

items.title: 📋 Copy lyrics — plain-text copy button
items.note: The only ways to get lyrics out of a package were the full JSON export or one short-form clip caption at a time. SongPackageView's existing rawLyrics (section labels + lines) is now one click via a 'Copy lyrics' button next to Explain/Export, same copied-confirmation pattern already used elsewhere in the file.

items.title: ⭐ Vault favorites — pin your best takes
items.note: A growing vault had no way to mark a keeper. New storage.ts favorites store (loadFavorites/toggleFavorite, its own localStorage key, best-effort like taste/alias, no bearing on generation or SongPackage's shape) + a ☆/⭐ toggle per VaultDrawer row, sorted favorites-first with a stable sort.

items.title: 🎤 Click-a-word rhyme helper
items.note: The read-only lyrics view had no way to check a word's rhyme options. Every word in Final Lyrics is now clickable, surfacing lexicon.ts's existing rhymesWith() (already built for generation) as a reference-only lookup — never edits the lyric.

items.title: 📏 Word/line/runtime counter
items.note: The Final Lyrics label had no sense of song length at a glance. A small counter now reads '183 words · 23 lines · ~2:00 (est.)' — word/line counts exact, runtime an explicitly-labeled rough estimate (2 bars/line at the production tempo, a rule of thumb, not a measurement).

items.title: 📑 Duplicate this song — fork a vault entry
items.note: No way to branch a version to try a wild edit without risking the one already liked. New storage.ts duplicateSong(id) clones into a fully independent entry — new id, version 1, title suffixed '(copy)' (bumped to '(copy 2)'... on repeat, never colliding). A 'duplicate' button on every VaultDrawer row.

items.title: 📝 Per-song vault notes
items.note: No way to leave yourself a reminder on a specific take ('needs a bridge rewrite', 'send to Marcus'). New storage.ts loadSongNotes()/setSongNote(id, text) — a Record<songId, string> in its own localStorage key, best-effort like favorites/taste, 280-char cap. An inline input per VaultDrawer row, saved on blur.

items.title: ⌨️ Cmd/Ctrl+Enter to generate
items.note: Submitting Song Lab required reaching for the mouse every time. A window-level keydown listener submits on Cmd/Ctrl+Enter from anywhere in the form (same convention as Slack/Discord), respecting the same briefReady guard the button uses.

items.title: 🎵 One-click Copy Suno prompt
items.note: The Suno-ready prompt (style of music + tagged lyrics) was only reachable by opening 'Explain this song' and finding it inside the full trace explorer. A direct 'Copy Suno prompt' button reuses suno.ts's existing sunoTrack() — no new generation logic, just a shorter path to something the brain already builds.

items.title: ⎋ Escape-key close for Vault/Album drawers
items.note: Both overlays only closed via the Close button or clicking the scrim. A window-level Escape listener (same pattern as Cmd/Ctrl+Enter) now closes each from the keyboard. Deliberately NOT added to the Lyric Lab — its in-progress free-write draft box makes Escape-to-close a data-loss risk, not a delight.

items.title: 📄 Markdown export
items.note: The only downloadable formats were full JSON (backup/re-import) or a Suno-specific prompt — nothing readable for Notion, GitHub, or a plain lyric sheet. New songMarkdown(pkg): title as H1, concept, brief, chosen hook as a blockquote, every section as an H3, production notes as a bullet list. Pure + deterministic. A new 'Export Markdown' download button next to Export JSON.

items.title: ↩️ Undo a committed Lyric Lab step
items.note: Back-navigation already let you revisit a step and commit a different answer, but there was no way to clear a step back to uncommitted without immediately picking a replacement. New uncommit() deletes the current step's log entry; an 'Undo' button in the committed box. Safe by construction — taste-recording only runs once, in finish(), off the final log state.

items.title: ↺ Reset to defaults on Song Lab
items.note: After trying Surprise Me / Load Example / a Pattern Pack, the only way back to blank was reloading the page. New resetForm() clears back to DEFAULTS; a 'Reset' button appears next to Surprise Me/Load Example, shown only once the form is actually dirty.

items.title: 🕐 Recently viewed strip in the Vault
items.note: No quick way back to a song opened a minute ago without scrolling the full list. New storage.ts loadRecentlyViewed()/recordRecentlyViewed(id) (newest-first, capped at 5, its own localStorage key), recorded on every vault-open, a chip strip above the main VaultDrawer list. A dark/light theme toggle was descoped in the same round after checking app/globals.css — fully dark-only, ~20 hardcoded color variables, no light-mode infrastructure; a real second palette is a design-system project, not a tiny build.

items.title: 🔎 Vault search/filter box
items.note: A growing vault had no way to jump straight to a title without scrolling. A 'Search by title…' input filters the visible list by case-insensitive substring match, shown only past 5 songs. An honest 'No songs match' empty state distinguishes an empty vault from a search with no hits.

items.title: ✎ Inline vault-song rename
items.note: No way to fix a typo or retitle a song without a full regenerate (new version) or duplicating it. New storage.ts renameSong(id, newTitle): an in-place metadata edit, version untouched. A pencil button per VaultDrawer row toggles an inline Save/Cancel rename. Caught + fixed a real Escape-key interaction bug with the earlier Escape-to-close-drawer feature (7.10) in the same PR — cancelling a rename was also closing the whole drawer until stopPropagation() was added to the rename input's Escape handler.

items.title: 🗑 Clear all avoid-words
items.note: A learned avoid-words list only ever grew, one word removed at a time — no fast way to wipe it after retiring an old artist alias or starting fresh. New clearAvoidWords(), gated by the same window.confirm() pattern already used for 'Restore from backup' (the only other destructive-action confirm in the app). A '🗑 clear all' button shown only when the list is non-empty; confirming resets it to 0 and the change persists via the existing saveBannedWords().

items.title: 📋 Copy JSON to clipboard
items.note: Export JSON only ever downloaded a file — pasting a package straight into a chat, an issue, or another tool meant downloading then re-opening it. New copyJson() mirrors the existing Copy lyrics/Copy Suno prompt pattern exactly (navigator.clipboard.writeText, a 1.6s 'JSON copied ✓' confirmation). A '📋 Copy JSON' button sits next to the existing '⬇ Export JSON' download button — the download stays for backup/re-import, the copy is for quick paste elsewhere.

items.title: / jumps to the Vault search box
items.note: The Vault search box (shipped in 7.15) had no keyboard shortcut — same GitHub/Slack convention missing that Cmd/Ctrl+Enter already brought to Song Lab. A window-level keydown listener focuses the search input on '/', but only when focus isn't already inside an input/textarea (rename box, note field), so a literal '/' typed anywhere else is never hijacked. Placeholder updated to '(press / to focus)' as the visible hint.

items.title: 🔽 Vault sort toggle (newest/oldest/title A–Z)
items.note: The vault only ever showed newest-first (storage.ts's listSongs() default) with no way to flip it. A 'Sort the vault' <select> (Newest first / Oldest first / Title A–Z) reorders the base list before the existing favorites-first stable sort runs on top, so favorites still always float to the top regardless of sort mode. Shown once there's more than 1 song. Note: while auditing this candidate a 'per-song share link' idea from the queue turned out to already be shipped — SongPackageView.tsx's shareSong()/🔗 Share button already covers it — so that candidate was dropped rather than duplicated.

items.title: 📋 Copy all lyrics — bulk clipboard export for the whole vault
items.note: Copying one song's lyrics at a time (7.2) doesn't help someone archiving the whole catalog at once. New copyAllLyrics() in VaultDrawer.tsx concatenates every song's title + [Section]-labeled lyrics (same format each song's own 'Copy lyrics' button uses), separated by '---', into one clipboard copy. A '📋 Copy all lyrics' button in the top control row, shown whenever the vault isn't empty.

items.title: 🔀 Duplicate + rename in one motion
items.note: Duplicate (7.6) and rename (7.16) were two separate clicks — fork, then hunt for the new '(copy)'-titled row and open its rename box. duplicateInVault() now returns the clone (storage.ts's duplicateSong() already did); a new duplicateAndRename() in VaultDrawer.tsx immediately opens the rename box on the fresh clone, pre-filled with its '(copy)' title, so renaming — the near-universal next step after forking — takes one click instead of two.

items.title: 📅 'N songs today' stat in the Vault header
items.note: The Vault header only ever showed a flat total count, no sense of momentum. A new todayCount compares each song's createdAt against new Date().toDateString() (a UI-level Date() use, not the generation path — same convention identity.ts/album.ts's genId() already established) and appends '· N songs today' to the header, hidden when the count is 0 so an idle day shows nothing extra.

items.title: 📄 Copy all as Markdown — bulk vault export, richer format
items.note: Copy all lyrics (7.21) only carries lyrics — someone archiving the whole vault might want the richer per-song format (concept, brief, hook, production notes) that a single song's own 'Export Markdown' button already produces via songMarkdown(). New copyAllMarkdown() reuses that exact formatter across every vault song, '---'-separated, into one clipboard copy. A '📄 Copy all as Markdown' button sits next to '📋 Copy all lyrics'.

items.title: 🗑 Clear all vault notes
items.note: Per-song notes (7.7) only ever cleared one at a time by blanking each field — no bulk reset, mirroring the gap clearAvoidWords() (7.17) closed for the avoid-words list. New storage.ts clearAllSongNotes() wipes the whole notes map; a confirm-gated '🗑 clear all notes' button in VaultDrawer.tsx, shown only when at least one note exists. +1 storage test.

phases.name: Medium features — the planned arc
phases.goal: Founder directive, 2026-07-03: stop the tiny-feature cadence after 7.25; plan and ship coherent, multi-PR capabilities instead. A research agent drafted six proposals (Producer's Chair, Talk to the Brain, Brief Interpreter, Artist HQ, Council load-bearing, Studio Flow); founder picked Studio Flow to start.

items.title: 🧭 Studio Flow PR1 — the Review/Refine/Keep/Release rail
items.note: Studio mode presented ~10 panels + an 8-button toolbar all at once with no guided path from 'song generated' to 'refined, kept, and released.' New flowStage state + a 4-tab rail (①Review ②Refine ③Keep ④Release) shown only once a song exists. Each tab is a focus state, not a wall — clicking one smooth-scrolls to and rings-highlights that stage's panels (Council + BangerScoreCard for Review, the lyrics editor for Refine, ArtistCard/Rack/Recommendations for Keep, the Song Package toolbar for Release) while every panel stays rendered and reachable, nothing hidden. Playwright-verified live: rail absent in compose mode, present after generating, default stage Review selected, each tab click sets aria-selected + the correct anchor's data-active + scrolls it into view, Keep-stage panel still visible while parked on Review (nothing hidden). Zero console errors. PR1 of ~3 — next up: the Release-desk regroup (PR2), then a mobile pass (PR3).

items.title: 🖥️ Agent Board upgrade — live connection lines + terminal signal ticker
items.note: Founder: 'it's literally them thinking' — the Agent Board was a static snapshot grid even though lib/hermes/nervousSystem.ts already models real Signal message-passing between regions (Signal, outgoingPathways()) that nothing displayed. New signalLog state in HermesHitFactory.tsx subscribes to the nervous system live; AgentBoard.tsx draws a short-lived animated SVG connector line between the two most-recently-fired agent cards whenever their regions share a real brainMap.ts pathway (no invented edges); a new SignalTicker.tsx renders the same signals as a retro terminal readout (monospace, traffic-light title bar, blinking cursor — founder explicitly asked for 'some sort of terminal look'). Caught and fixed a real gap while testing: loadDemo() (the 'See a finished example' button — the most common first-touch path) bypassed run() entirely and never fired any signals, so the new ticker/lines stayed empty on that path; fixed by replaying the demo song's own already-computed agentOutputs through the same signalForAgent()/nervous-system path run() uses, so the showcase is an honest replay, not empty. Playwright-verified live: ticker shows real signal lines (not the idle placeholder) after both the demo path and a real generation, terminal cursor blinks, at least one connection line renders during agent-by-agent playback. Zero console errors.

items.title: 🔌 Council-voice-registry refactor — the plug-in prerequisite
items.note: Founder: 'if we were to connect another panel or another council into this... come up with three ideas that would make this take #1 on the App Store' — audited the code and confirmed there was no real extension point: AgentId (types.ts) is a closed 10-agent union and COUNCIL_WEIGHTS (council.ts) was a hardcoded 3-4-voice object. rankHooksByCouncil() now takes an optional 5th guestVoices: CouncilVoice[] parameter — each a {id, label, weight, score(ctx)} plug-in that claims a capped share (MAX_GUEST_SHARE = 50%) of the final councilScore, split proportionally among however many guests are attached, so the built-in board never loses the majority verdict. With no guest voices supplied the computation collapses back to the exact original expression — byte-identical to before this existed (proven by +6 new tests plus all 15 pre-existing council tests passing unchanged). This is the prerequisite for Guest Judges (8.4) and Agent Packs; Council.tsx itself is untouched in this PR since nothing consumes the new parameter yet.

items.title: 🎭 Guest Judges — pluggable Council personas
items.note: The first real consumer of the 8.3 voice registry. Three deterministic, pure personas built on real lexicon/craft data (never invented randomness, never network): The A&R Exec (rewards a tight 4-8 word radio-hook length blended with the existing craft/crave score), The TikTok Algorithm (rewards brevity + motion/street/body imagery from lexicon.ts), Your Mom (rewards positive-affect, family/hope/light-imagery language via lexicon.ts's wordInfo()). Toggleable chips in Council.tsx seat 0+ guests for the session only (not persisted — a deliberate per-session choice); the ranking weight-summary line names whichever guests are seated so the score's composition stays transparent. +8 new tests proving each persona's distinct bias (Your Mom flips warm-vs-dark, TikTok flips short-vs-long) and that scores stay in [0,100]/deterministic. Playwright-verified live: all three chips render, aria-pressed toggles correctly, the weight summary correctly names a seated guest and reverts when un-seated. Zero console errors.

items.title: 🎛️ Agent Packs MVP — genre/scene Council voice bundles
items.note: The second consumer of the 8.3 voice registry, seated alongside Guest Judges via the same mechanism. Scoped down from the original 'new agent + new Council voice' framing in IDEAS.md: adding a genuinely new pipeline AGENT would mean extending the closed AgentId union threaded through the deterministic core (types.ts/agents.ts/pipeline.ts/brainMap.ts REGIONS) — too large and risky for an MVP, and not needed to deliver the real value. Instead: three genre/scene-flavored Council voices, same pure/deterministic/lexicon-grounded discipline as Guest Judges — Boom-Bap Traditionalist (rewards street/time imagery + an 8-word-ish length), Pop Radio (rewards intra-hook word repetition + brevity), Poetry Slam (rewards lexicon-hit density + imagery-tag diversity — the only judge that rewards richness over brevity). Council.tsx renders a second chip row beneath Guest Judges; both sets combine freely (SEATABLE = [...GUEST_JUDGES, ...AGENT_PACKS]), sharing one weight-summary line. +9 new tests proving each pack's distinct bias and combinability. Playwright-verified live: all three chips render, toggle correctly, combine with a Guest Judge in the weight summary, and clear correctly when un-seated (root-caused one test false-positive along the way — a static hint line's own copy contained the word 'seated', not a real bug — confirmed via the dynamic weight-summary text itself, which correctly went '+ Pop Radio, Your Mom seated' -> '+ Your Mom seated' -> gone).

items.title: 📖 Word ideas — a similar-words popup while editing lyrics
items.note: Founder: 'polishing off the lyric area and editing... an inline dictionary/thesaurus... really easy edit ability.' New lexicon.ts similarWords(word) — words sharing the target's imagery category, ranked by closeness of affect, honestly framed as 'similar in feel' rather than a strict thesaurus (the lexicon carries no synonym data; same reference-only spirit as the existing rhymesWith()). Wired into ScribeEditor.tsx (the line-by-line lyric editor): double-click any word in a line to open a popup of similar words; click one to replace the double-clicked word in place via the native input selectionStart/selectionEnd. +5 new lexicon tests (imagery match, empty for unknown words, deterministic, max cap, case-insensitive). Playwright-verified live end to end: double-clicking 'hold' in a real generated song's line surfaced 10 real similar words (sight, brain, mind, feel, tight, frame, mark, sound, heart, steel — all sharing the 'body' imagery tag), clicking one ('sight') correctly replaced the word in the line and dismissed the popup. Scoped to ScribeEditor's line inputs only per founder's 'while editing' framing — the read-only rhyme-helper view already has its own click-a-word tool; a follow-up (8.7) makes edits ripple through the rest of the panel (scores/uniqueness), which today only updates on save.

items.title: 🔄 Live re-scoring on lyric edit — the panel ripples with an edit
items.note: Founder: 'that needs to be recognized throughout the rest of the globally. The rest of the panel has to be linked up to what's going on.' Confirmed the exact gap by reading saveLyricEdit: it only ever updated finalLyrics + sections, leaving Banger Score/Uniqueness Report/viral clips computed from the ORIGINAL generation, stale after any edit. Fixed by re-deriving everything downstream of the lyrics through the same pure functions the pipeline itself uses — checkOriginality(), scoreSong(), emotionClarity(), and a newly-exported buildClips() (previously private to pipeline.ts) — replayed against the edited text on every save. Council's ranking needed no fix: it already reads pkg.sections fresh on every render. Playwright-verified live: replaced a real song's lyrics with drastically different (short, repetitive, off-theme) text and confirmed the Banger Score visibly dropped (99 -> 75) and the word/line count label updated, proving the whole panel now ripples with an edit instead of only the text box.

items.title: 🧭 Guided tour of the Scribe lyric editor (coach-marks)
items.note: Founder: research how Scribe (scribehow.com — auto-converts a real workflow into an annotated step-by-step guide) works and bring that into the lyrical area; clarified via AskUserQuestion to mean a guided onboarding tour of the editor itself, not a songwriting-replay feature. New generic GuidedTour.tsx — a small, dependency-free coach-mark overlay driven by a static {selector, title, body}[] config, spotlighting one real DOM element at a time (box-shadow cutout technique) with Next/Skip/Done navigation. Five steps teach ScribeEditor's actual affordances: editing a line directly, the double-click word-ideas popup (8.6), the AI-rewrite sparkle, add-line, delete-line — anchored via data-tour attributes on the first rendered line only. Shown automatically the first time a browser opens the editor (new storage.ts hasSeenScribeTour()/markScribeTourSeen(), a plain one-time flag, not .bak-mirrored); replayable anytime via a '? Show me around' button. +2 new storage tests. Playwright-verified live end to end: auto-shows on first visit, all 5 steps step through via Next, Done closes it and marks it seen, a reload + re-open does NOT auto-show it again, and the manual replay button + Skip both work correctly.

items.title: 🕸️ Agent Lifecycle library — data layer only, no UI wired up yet
items.note: Foundry-inspired BeaconState + selector pattern for the agent brain: buildAgentLifecycleState(songs, opts?) aggregates every agent's contributions across the whole vault into persistent AgentProfile records (totalContributions, collaborators, milestones) plus a collaborationGraph social graph, with selectors (selectTopAgentsByContribution, selectAgentByIdWithCollaborators, selectCollaborationNetwork, selectAgentStats) as pure projections over that state. Landed as a pure, deterministic (opts.now-injected, no bare new Date()/Math.random() in the generation path) core module only — a first review round caught real bugs before any UI shipped: two colliding CSS class names in the also-added dashboard block (.agentCard, .label, .panelTitle vs. hermes.module.css's existing rules, silently reskinning 17+ live components), an asymmetric collaboration graph that left later-iterated agents with an empty collaborators list, an empty-array truthiness bug that skipped the agentOutputs:[] legacy-song fallback, and an always-0-or-1 totalSongs stat. All fixed; zero callers anywhere in the repo yet by design — next PR wires an AgentLifecycleDashboard component (roster grid + per-agent detail + collaboration table) on top of this data layer, using the now-collision-free dashboard CSS block.

items.title: 🎨 WIFI DJ visual redesign — directory + phased plan
items.note: Founder-supplied 'Production UI Kit' mockups (Desktop/Mobile Studio + 24-element component library, assets/concept-art/wifi-dj-production-ui-kit-desktop-mobile.png) plus a separate Lyrics Editor mockup (assets/concept-art/wifi-dj-lyrics-editor.png) reimagine the whole Hit Factory UI. Three parallel research agents mapped every button/panel in both mockups and audited the existing codebase (routes, HermesHitFactory.tsx's 3-column deck + Studio Flow rail, BottomNav.tsx, Council.tsx, LyricLab.tsx, Studio.tsx, Rack.tsx, BrainScan.tsx, YourAgent.tsx, hermes.module.css/globals.css tokens) to produce a cross-referenced directory in docs/wifi-dj-redesign.md — every mockup element marked as an existing match, a restyle, or net-new. Key findings: BottomNav.tsx is already a near-1:1 match to the mobile dock; Council.tsx's .flag rows are the closest match to the mockup's 'Council Card' component; LyricLab.tsx is a step-wizard, structurally unlike the new inline Lyrics Editor (net-new build); the DAW mixer/track-lane concept has no real multi-stem-audio backing today (flagged as a UI metaphor to build honestly, same spirit as the Brain Scan honesty rule, not a literal audio-stem claim). Decided mobile-first build order (matches the existing device-capability-flag responsive system already in place). Phased plan: Phase 1 Council redesign + new logo (founder's explicit first target, in progress), Phase 2 shared primitives (Toggle/Segmented Tab/Status Badge/Notification Pill/Progress Ring/Slide-out Card), Phase 3 sidebar nav shell + BottomNav reskin, Phase 4 Lyrics Editor rebuild, Phase 5 Studio/mixer visual layer. Each phase ships as its own PR, screenshot-reviewed against the mockup by an independent agent (target 9/10+, escalate to founder only after 5 failed rounds on one slice).

items.title: 🎨 Council redesign — Phase 1, shipped as interim
items.note: Phase 1 of 8.10. Two independent design agents proposed a Council-Card-style visual language (converged closely — glowing avatar-ringed cards, filled+glowing seat chips, a violet-tinted verdict card, a diamond-mark 'WiFi DJ' logo lockup in the panel header); implemented, then run through 5 build→screenshot→independent-review cycles against the mockup (scores: 6 → 8 → 8 → 8 → 8/10). A desktop specialist and a mobile specialist each did a dedicated polish pass (real per-card ambient glow via color-mix() on the bench tint, filled/glowing seat chips, tap-target/glow-radius/density tuning at ≤640px) between rounds 2 and 3. Never cleared the founder's 9/10 bar — per his explicit instruction ('after the fifth attempt stop and ask me for help'), stopped after round 5 and escalated rather than continuing to iterate blind. Founder's answer: 'ship 8/10 as interim.' Merged via PR #187. Remaining gaps from the final review, left as future polish rather than blockers: a green/teal hue crept into the Challenges-bench cards and default chips/badges that isn't in the mockup's cyan/magenta/amber vocabulary; amber only has one foothold (the Agent Pack chips) instead of appearing throughout the way the mockup uses it; the two hemisphere hues are split across two different card types rather than blended within one card face the way the mockup's Council Card does it.

items.title: 🕸️ Self-expanding UI-agent infrastructure + first de-gray sweep
items.note: Founder directive: 'I don't want you actually doing work you're handing any work off... if you see something that needs to be done and there's not an agent created for it then you create that gap and the knowledge that you create, it's logged in the memory layer.' Created a persistent hermes-ui subagent charter (reads brain/uiDesignLanguage.json's hardRules + knownGapsBacklog before every change, appends agentLearningsLog after) instead of doing the 'no grey ingredients' UI work directly. First dispatch (background, general-purpose agent following the charter by instruction, since new .claude/agents/*.md types need a session restart to register) shipped all 3 queued gaps: header nav split into .navPrimary (near-solid cyan→violet gradient pill) / .navUtility (single-hue tinted panel, data-tone="danger" for Sign out) tiers replacing one undifferentiated .ghostBtn; the 6 real <select> dropdowns (corrected from an earlier '~15' estimate) got a dark-fill/glowing-cyan-border/custom-chevron treatment matching the mockup's actual Filter Dropdown/Key Display tiles (re-inspected rather than assumed); BottomNav.tsx got a real active-state (Council picked-rank-badge gradient+glow) driven by a new `active` prop computed from vaultOpen/mode/flowStage. Logged one new hard-rule refinement (not every mockup element is a bright gradient — some are deliberately a calmer single-hue glow) and one new gap (custom-select-listbox: the native OS popup can't be restyled past the trigger) directly into the memory layer, plus a note that Crossroads' new data-active wiring is currently inert (CrossroadsBoard.tsx renders its own separate header). Full gate suite green (tsc, test:web 602 passed, node --test 33 passed, lint:check, STATIC_EXPORT build, eval, mobile-matrix 18/18).

phases.name: Kudbee TDE / HERMES Workbench
phases.goal: Founder directive, 2026-07-05: build a side cockpit inside the app for Kudbee agents, repos, memory, models, GPU jobs, training rows, PRs, SCRIBE, and future local coding-agent execution. Strictly suggest-only / mock-state / read-only in v1 — no live APIs, no keys, no execution, no changes to existing routes. One feat/tde-NN branch at a time; the full plan is docs/kudbee-tde-roadmap.md.

items.title: 🗺️ TDE Branch 00 — roadmap + spine sync
items.note: The branch-by-branch plan: 13 branches (00 roadmap → 12 backend-bridge plan), branch naming, PR/safety checklists, model routing rules, agent depth-1 rules, memory/training capture rules, and the live-execution gate that keeps everything mocked until a founder-approved permission layer exists. Docs only, no UI.

items.title: 🧱 TDE Branch 01 — /tde shell route
items.note: Isolated route with title, "Suggest-only prototype" badge, empty slots for the five panels (Mission / Repos / Agents / Models-GPU / Memory-Training), and a no-live-execution safety note. Own tde.module.css (never hermes.module.css — class-collision lesson, 8.9).

items.title: 🎯 TDE Branch 02 — Mission panel
items.note: Mission input + type selector (Code / SCRIBE / Training / GPU Eval / Memory / GitHub PR / Visual-Creative), mock submit, mock queued-mission cards. Suggest-only, nothing executes.

items.title: 📦 TDE Branch 03 — Repo panel
items.note: Mock repo cards: kudbee-music (this app), hermes-lyric-server (external backend/model repo), kudbee-engine (archived planning) — canonical branch, role, protected paths, status, integration status. External repos labeled as such.

items.title: 🕸️ TDE Branch 04 — Agent map panel
items.note: Main Agent + Training Scribe, Safety Reviewer, Eval Runner, Memory Agent, Future Integration Scout: status, role, allowed/forbidden actions, spawned_by, risk level, mock trace. Visible rule: sub-agent depth 1 only, recursive spawning blocked.

items.title: 🧠 TDE Branch 05 — Model/GPU panel
items.note: Model router cards (KUDBEECODEV0-MINI/14B, KUDBEESCRIBEV1, KUDBEEV1, HERMES, embeddings, Mistral 7B legacy lane), T4 vs RTX 6000 hardware-mode cards, mock GPU job queue, next-GPU-action card.

items.title: 📚 TDE Branch 06 — Memory/Training panel
items.note: Memory/state cards (PROJECT_STATE.md, ARCHITECTURE_TRUTH.md, agent-state-tracker, repo atlas), training row count, eval pass rate, drop queue, next data target, next-RTX-command placeholder. Mock values seeded from real project facts, clearly marked mode: mock.

items.title: 🚧 TDE Branch 07 — Safety gate UI
items.note: The visible blocked-actions list: root git add -A, wrong-repo edits, wrong-branch push, deleting final//model caches, committing secrets/artifacts, CPU training fallback, recursive spawning, unapproved live API calls.

items.title: 🧭 TDE Branch 08 — Mock model router
items.note: Typed mock routing rules: task type → recommended model/tool + reason + risk level + approval-required flag. No live calls.

items.title: 📄 TDE Branch 09 — Integration contracts
items.note: TypeScript types only for future integrations: RepoStatus, AgentStatus, ModelStatus, GPUJobStatus, MemoryState, TrainingEvalSummary, PRStatus, ScribeStatus. No fetching.

items.title: 💾 TDE Branch 10 — Local mock persistence
items.note: Persist mock missions in the visitor's own localStorage following storage.ts conventions (sanitized imports, length caps). No secrets, no credentials, no execution.

items.title: ✨ TDE Branch 11 — Polish + navigation
items.note: Nav link only if safe, responsive/mobile stacking (mobile-matrix), empty/loading states, WIFI DJ design-language polish (fill+glow, locked palette).

items.title: 🌉 TDE Branch 12 — Backend bridge plan
items.note: Docs only: the permission layer required before any live execution — GitHub, hermes-server health, Lightning job status, memory/vector, SCRIBE endpoint, KUDBEECODEV0 eval scoreboard integrations, and the safety gates each must pass.

videoStudioTrack.note: The music-video studio's engineering backlog (studio/*.mjs) — formerly a hand checklist in BUILD_LOG.md, now governed by the spine like everything else. The Runway adapter lives under ecosystem.runwayGen4.

items.title: hermes new — project scaffold (song/, assets/, hermes.json, lyrics.md)
items.note: BUILD_LOG iteration 1

items.title: Scene packs vhs-lofi + lyric-minimal (4 packs total)
items.note: BUILD_LOG iteration 2; verified rendered slices

items.title: Project-targeted build — hermes build <dir> via HERMES_DATA
items.note: BUILD_LOG iteration 3; flagship build stays byte-identical

items.title: Two-hemisphere brain model + --brain dial + left-brain eval gate (hermes qa)
items.note: BUILD_LOG iteration 4; CI smoke job renders + gates a real slice

items.title: Audio-novelty song-structure detection (segment from beats + energy when lyrics.md has no headers)
items.note: today sections come from lyrics.md headers only (verified 2026-07-02)

items.title: hermes-composer — optional MusicGen wiring (opt-in, documented heavy deps)
items.note: no studio file references MusicGen yet (verified 2026-07-02)

items.title: Per-pack scene variety for generic projects (more than the shared scene cycle)
items.note: generic projects currently share one scenePool cycle regardless of --pack

items.title: Right-brain variance — a --seed so the right hemisphere generates scene variants and qa picks the best
items.note: --seed exists only in studio/runway.mjs today (unrelated Runway param)

ecosystem.note: Bigger initiatives that integrate with the engine via API — kept OUT of the free local core so it stays $0. Tracked here so they don't get lost; built when the founder greenlights.

workersBuildsCheck.status: resolved — founder deleted the stray Worker (2026-07-02)
workersBuildsCheck.what: History: a stray Workers service named kudbee-music was git-connected to this repo and failed a 'Workers Builds' check + posted a ❌ bot comment on every push for ~30 PRs before being tracked (#111) and deleted by the founder the same day. If a stray check ever reappears, track it immediately (CLAUDE.md never-skip-silently rule).

crossroadsBoard.stage1: SHIPPED (#44): lib/hermes/crossroads.ts (openCrossing/vote/tally/leader/decide, pure+immutable) + brain/crossroads.json (3 seeded crossings) + tests. The $0/local decision model.
crossroadsBoard.stage2: SHIPPED (#116): a /crossroads route (components/hermes/CrossroadsBoard.tsx) renders every seeded crossing (question, ranked options + rationale + a vote bar, front-runner readout); clicking an option casts/changes THIS browser's own vote via lib/hermes/crossroadsStorage.ts (localStorage-only, same BYOK-key idiom as claudeKey.ts) — no account, no server. lib/hermes/crossroadsBoard.ts keeps the seed+vote combine logic pure/tested. Honest about scope: the tally is the seeded base plus this browser's one vote, not a real community headcount (that's stage 4). Linked from the Hit Factory header; /crossroads added to the mobile device-matrix.
crossroadsBoard.stage3: SHIPPING (this PR): Signals → the brain — board decisions feed the taste/memory model (recordTaste) and steer generation. Each crossing option now carries a tasteSignal: { liked, disliked } metadata (e.g., voting for 'soul/gospel' adds 'soulful', 'uplifting', 'church', 'triumphant' to your taste). New function castVoteAndRecordTaste() combines voting + taste recording. The UI shows '· shapes taste' hint on options with signals. When you decide at a crossroads, your taste updates, and the next song reflects your choice — the loop closes.
crossroadsBoard.what: The WIFI DJ governance + community steering surface — the brain's 'decision' region made social. The community and the agents meet at the crossroads to steer creative + ecosystem direction.
plan: 1. Board model (local-first, $0): brain/crossroads.json + lib/hermes/crossroads.ts — a data model of 'crossings' (a decision/fork: creative choice, persona/pack addition, direction). Each crossing has options, rationales, weighted votes, and an outcome. Versioned in git, same pattern as beliefs/personas.
2. The Board UI: a /crossroads route rendering active crossings — right-hemisphere proposals vs left-hemisphere challenges vs the artist's decision (shares the Council + Brain Scan aesthetic).
3. Signals → the brain: board decisions feed the taste/memory model (recordTaste) and can steer generation, exactly like Lyric Lab choices. ✅ SHIPPING (this PR)
4. Community layer (opt-in, via API): proposals/votes sync to a backend (Supabase / Cloudflare D1); identity via email or wallet. No secrets in the core repo.

5. Web3 governance (later, via API): token-weighted voting / Solana / NFT membership steers the G-DJ. The Board is the on-ramp; the token layer plugs in through the API, never bloating the free local core.
crossroadsBoard.guardrail: Ships in stages; stages 1–3 are $0/local and live in this repo. Stages 4–5 are a separate service the core calls via API.

lightningAI.what: Per-agent compute for the opt-in 'advance your model' tier: a user signs up and gets their own agent; paid tiers give a bigger brain.
lightningAI.assessment: Great fit for the OPTIONAL GPU / 'bigger brain' lane and for prototyping per-agent compute — NOT the backbone. The base experience stays $0/local/serverless; the real prerequisite for 'their own agent' is an auth + DB layer (accounts + persistent vault), which is compute-light. Recommend testing Lightning as one opt-in provider behind the existing LyricsProvider seam, reversible and low-risk.
lightningAI.spike: DONE (adapter half AND live end-to-end, 2026-07-04): studio/lightning.mjs — a key-gated CLI adapter that POSTs a prompt to a LitServe / Lightning Studios HTTPS endpoint and extracts the generated lyrics, unit-tested with an injected fetch (test/lightning.test.mjs), reading LIGHTNING_ENDPOINT + LIGHTNING_API_KEY only from gitignored .env.local (server/CLI-side, never NEXT_PUBLIC_). Mirrors studio/runway.mjs. The founder stood up one Lightning Studio (Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct behind a LitServe HTTPS endpoint) and both `node studio/lightning.mjs --ping` and a real `--prompt` call round-tripped successfully, returning generated lyrics — the adapter contract is proven live, not just unit-tested. Also learned: prompting an LLM to hold a rhyme scheme (AABB) is unreliable — an explicit prompt with bracketed section tags and a banned-cliche list fixed the first couplet but not the second, the known gap HERMES's own engine avoids by guaranteeing rhyme by construction (lib/hermes/rhyme.ts) rather than asking nicely. This motivated a LoRA fine-tuning smoke test on the founder's own separate Lightning-Studio project (not part of this repo) — out of scope here beyond this note. REMAINING: the visitor-facing BYOK Lightning slot in the Engine Rack — a visitor pastes their OWN endpoint/key client-side, mirroring lib/hermes/claudeKey.ts (see docs/lightning-plan.md's two-tier key model). Not yet built; no UI change ships until then.
lightningAI.prerequisite: Accounts + persistent vault (see Phase 4 durability) — 'their own agent' needs identity + storage first.

livingBrainNft.nearTerm: SHIPPED (#45): lib/hermes/brainSignature.ts — brainSignature() → deterministic traits (dominant hemisphere, temperature, signature rhyme, songs made, becoming-you, primary emotion) + toNftMetadata() → Metaplex Token Metadata off-chain JSON (#85 aligned it to the stated Solana target — 32-char name clamp, symbol, properties.files, seller_fee_basis_points: 0; see docs/nft-standard.md). $0, no chain, no network — the exact JSON a token points to. Any future mint: mpl-token-metadata/Umi, devnet-first, mainnet only on explicit founder approval.
livingBrainNft.what: The particle brain as a DYNAMIC NFT that evolves as you create. tokenURI → metadata URL whose animation_url is the live Brain-Scan HTML + a snapshot image; traits = dominant hemisphere / temperature / top rhyme sound / songs-made / becoming-you% / primary emotion. Value = utility (unlocks compute tier + Crossroads votes + roles + radio/curation), evolving status/provenance, portable G-DJ identity, song royalties. NEAR-TERM $0 STEP (buildable, pairs with the Particle Brain): brainSignature() → deterministic traits + a stable metadata JSON + a shareable /brain/[id] render URL (no chain yet). Chain mint (Solana/Metaplex) = founder decision. Guardrail: free core stays free; utility/identity framing, not investment promises.

discordServer.what: Community server (preferred over Telegram) to learn-the-process + contribute. Plan: channels (#showcase/#help/#contributing/#song-drops), roles, voice listening parties, and a GitHub→Discord webhook posting commits/PRs/releases. Draft structure + wire the webhook on the founder's go-ahead; optional Telegram announce mirror later.

wifiDjRadio.what: A live radio streaming engine-made music; community submits + shapes; eventually each person runs their own agent. FOUNDER (2026-07-03): "WIFI radio will have like a jukebox songs. People can submit songs. We have contest stuff — bring more value." Concretely: a jukebox/playlist surface where the community submits finished HERMES songs, plus contest mechanics (voting, winners, featured drops) — natural extension of the Crossroads Board's vote/tally model (crossroadsBoard above) applied to song submissions instead of governance crossings, and of the Vault/Song-Gifts share-link infra for getting a song from one artist's browser onto a shared surface. Not started — needs the community layer (crossroadsBoard step 4, an opt-in backend) before submissions can be shared across visitors rather than staying local to one browser.

token.what: Crossroads / Solana / token / NFT governance — funds the ecosystem, powers community steering; integrates via API.

perAgentCompute.what: See lightningAI — each agent on its own minimal compute, paid tiers to advance the model.

landingPage.what: Interactive scroll-driven landing at / (components/landing/): scrubbed hero over reused repo clips, hemispheres, demo-proof table, CTA to /hermes. Live: https://wifi-dj-meme.pages.dev (Cloudflare Pages project wifi-dj-meme; wifidj.xyz aliasing = founder). Runway hero + wallet connect remain founder-gated comment slots.

publicReadiness.what: SECURITY.md (threat model, vulnerability reporting, paid-provider deployment rules: server-side keys + rate limits + spend caps, mock un-bypassable default) + least-privilege CI token. Founder toggles still open: branch protection on main (require PR + code-owner review), secret scanning + push protection, private vulnerability reporting.

identityLayer.what: Onboarding/identity layer: WelcomeGate + lib/hermes/identity.ts (local-first profiles under hermes.profile.v1). Guest accounts now; honest Google/GitHub OAuth slots (authProviders() reads NEXT_PUBLIC_AUTH_*, beginOAuth() throws until a real provider is wired — no fake sign-in); developer entry via /hermes?dev=1 (persisted hermes.devDoor.v1) so testing needs no login. Song Lab defaults are now EMPTY (no preloaded words) with a one-click example-brief loader + generate gating. Founder to activate real OAuth: pick hosted auth (Supabase/Auth0/Clerk) or Cloudflare Pages Functions + register Google/GitHub apps + set NEXT_PUBLIC_AUTH_* (docs/accounts.md). Per-profile/cloud vault = Phase 4.

docsSite.what: Astro Starlight docs on GitHub Pages.

videoStudio.what: De-emphasized in the web app (focus on lyrics + brain). Code intact for the CLI; re-enable later.

runwayGen4.what: studio/runway.mjs (hermes runway) drives Runway's image_to_video (Gen-4 Turbo) behind process.env.RUNWAY_API_KEY (key in .env.local, gitignored, NEVER committed; committed .env.example placeholder). Opt-in, key-gated, NEVER in the free core. Live-tested end-to-end with a real 10s clip animating assets/hero-still.png on the founder's test-account key/credits. Remaining, founder-paced (see docs/runway-plan.md): (1) animate the agent avatars into living characters, (2) generate the HERMES Studio 'office/living world' scenes (agents get bodies), (3) wire a clip into the actual landing-page hero, (4) real video-studio clips.
